data.title: RED: Write failing tests for sprint-next-batch.sh unlimited and 0 --limit handling
data.description:
## Description

Write failing tests for sprint-next-batch.sh --limit parameter edge cases: unlimited sentinel and 0 (empty batch).

### Files to create
- tests/scripts/test-sprint-next-batch-limit.sh

### Test cases (behavioral — run script, assert output)
- test_limit_unlimited: --limit=unlimited → returns full pool (no cap applied), output does not contain BATCH_SIZE: 0
- test_limit_zero: --limit=0 → returns BATCH_SIZE: 0 immediately with no TASK: lines
- test_limit_positive: --limit=3 → caps batch to 3 (existing behavior, regression guard)
- test_limit_omitted: no --limit flag → returns full pool (unlimited default)

### TDD requirement
Tests for unlimited and 0 must FAIL because sprint-next-batch.sh does not handle these values yet.

### Test approach
Each test runs sprint-next-batch.sh against a test epic with known tasks, passing different --limit values, and asserts on BATCH_SIZE output and TASK line count.

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test-sprint-next-batch-limit.sh (create)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-sprint-next-batch-limit.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-sprint-next-batch-limit.sh
- [ ] Test file contains at least 3 test cases
  Verify: grep -c "_test_\|test_" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-sprint-next-batch-limit.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 3)}'
- [ ] Tests for unlimited and 0 fail (RED)
  Verify: ! bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-sprint-next-batch-limit.sh 2>/dev/null
- [ ] All other existing tests still pass
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/run-all.sh
